articles.title: Hidden Game: All hail rookie goaltender Jacob Fowler in comeback victory at Ottawa
articles.description: Montreal 3, Ottawa 2. Freshly called up, the Canadiens' No. 3 goalie shuts down frantic Senators in final minutes.

articles.title: "Misalignment," NHL goalie expert says is Edmonton Oilers goalie's big problem
articles.description: This in from NHL goalie expert Steve Valiquette, long-time hockey analyst for MSG Networks in New York and CEO of Clear Sight Analytics, his take on what’s gone wrong with new Edmonton Oilers goalie Tristan Jarry. articles.title: Ullmark allows two stinkers in gut-punch loss to Habs
articles.description: Linus Ullmark had an .870 save percentage, Thomas Chabot couldn't elevate his game on the top pairing, Drake Batherson scored twice and Tim Stutzle had two assists for the Ottawa Senators in a 3-2 loss to the Montreal Canadiens.

articles.title: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
articles.description: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.

articles.title: Niagara Region chair Bob Gale resigns over allegations he owns signed Adolf Hitler book
articles.description: Bob Gale has resigned as the chair of the Regional Municipality of Niagara amid reports he owns a copy of Mein Kampf signed by Adolf Hitler. articles.title: Bodies of two Chinese backpackers found in Australian floodwaters
articles.description: The pair had been reported missing on Tuesday after failing to reach their destination.

articles.title: Chinese brain interface startup Gestala raises $21M just two months after launch
articles.description: This is the largest early-stage funding in China’s brain computer interface industry.

articles.title: Manitoba government plans steps toward reducing nurse overtime, improving care
articles.description: The Manitoba government is planning to set up two committees as part of its promise to improve health care.

articles.title: Brent crude oil briefly tops $100 a barrel as Iran attacks on shipping worsen supply concerns
articles.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The price of a barrel of Brent crude oil briefly topped $100 a barrel early Thursday, just days after it spiked near $120 in the latest jolts to financial markets and the global economy as a whole. articles.title: Man in his 80s seriously injured after fall, possible ‘electrocution’ in Longueuil
articles.description: An octogenarian is in critical condition Wednesday after falling to the ground in Longueuil. The man may have been “electrocuted,” according to a statement posted on social media by the Longueuil Police Department (SPAL). articles.title: Kelowna city council to vote on controversial golf course land swap on Monday
articles.description: If approved, the land swap between the city and a development company would see a portion of the Kelowna Springs Golf Course on Penno Road industrialized.

articles.title: Demidov scores game-winner, Canadiens beat Senators 3-2
articles.description: Ivan Demidov scored the game-winner to lead the Montreal Canadiens to a 3-2 win over the Ottawa Senators on Wednesday. Demidov also had an assist in the game. articles.title: Quit fossil fuels to stem deadly floods in Brazil’s coffee heartland, say scientists
articles.description: Global heating linked to rising risk of extreme rain that causes devastating landslides and rising coffee pricesThe record floods that have brought death and destruction to the heartland of Brazil’s coffee industry are expected to intensify if people continue to burn fossil fuels, analysis has shown

articles.title: Edmonton police seek dashcam footage of 122 Ave collision injuring 2 teens
articles.description: Edmonton police (EPS) are seeking dashcam footage of Tuesday’s collision, in which two teenagers were injured. articles.title: B.C. woman says her bedridden mother was sexually assaulted in care home
articles.description: A northern B.C. woman says her mother, who is bedridden with a debilitating disease, was sexually assaulted inside her care home last month.

articles.title: Edmonton bike group raises concern over shared use path during LRT line construction on 102 Ave
articles.description: An Edmonton cycling group is sounding the alarm over the relocation of a downtown bike lane to allow for the Valley Line LRT construction.
